content:
How to use: Select all text below the line, copy (Ctrl+C), and paste directly into LinkedIn's post editor. Line breaks will be preserved.
Every time I've changed projects or roles, I spend too much time rebuilding my environment, my workflow, my tools. I wanted something portable. Something I could carry wherever I go. So about 30 days ago, I started building corvia. An organizational memory system for AI agents, written in Rust. I wanted to learn generative AI firsthand by building in it, and I wanted to build something real. AI agents are developing memory. CLAUDE.md, Cursor rules, Copilot. These work for individual developers. But they're personal memory. What happens when agents need to share knowledge across repos? When decisions need to be tracked over time? That's organizational memory. That's the layer corvia is building. But there was a catch. The memory system didn't exist yet. So I recorded every design decision in markdown files by hand. The plan was to migrate everything once the tool could hold its own knowledge.
